name: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)
description: The Chinese Government Scholarship is a comprehensive scholarship program established by the Ministry of Education of China to support international students pursuing higher education in China.
eligibility: Open to all international students. Bachelor applicants must have high school diploma with good academic records. Master applicants must have bachelor degree. PhD applicants must have master degree. Age limits: Under 25 for bachelor, under 35 for master, under 40 for PhD. Good health and valid passport required.
coverage: Full tuition waiver, free on-campus accommodation, comprehensive medical insurance, monthly stipend (Bachelor: ¥2, 500/month, Master: ¥3, 000/month, PhD: ¥3, 500/month)
required_documents: ["Valid passport","Notarized highest diploma","Academic transcripts","Study plan (800+ words)","Two recommendation letters","Language proficiency certificate (Chinese or English)","Physical examination form","No criminal record certificate"]
application_process:
1. Check eligibility requirements on CSC website
2. Select your preferred Chinese university
3. Apply to the university for pre-admission
4. Prepare required documents
5. Submit online application via CSC portal
6. Wait for university nomination
7. Receive scholarship decision (usually by July)

name: Provincial Government Scholarships
description: Provincial Government Scholarships are offered by various Chinese provincial governments to attract international students to study in their regions. Coverage and benefits vary by province.
eligibility: Requirements vary by province. Generally open to all degree levels with good academic records. Some provinces may prioritize students from Belt and Road countries or sister cities.
coverage: Varies by province. Typically includes partial to full tuition waiver, and may include accommodation subsidies or monthly stipend.
required_documents: ["Valid passport","Academic transcripts","Diploma certificates","Recommendation letters","Study plan","Language certificate"]
application_process:
1. Research which provinces offer scholarships
2. Check specific provincial requirements
3. Apply to universities in target province
4. Submit scholarship application as per provincial guidelines
5. University nominates qualified students
6. Provincial education department reviews and approves

name: Silk Road Scholarship
description: The Silk Road Scholarship is established to support students from Belt and Road Initiative countries to study in China, promoting educational and cultural exchanges.
eligibility: Priority given to students from Belt and Road countries. Open to all academic levels. Applicants must meet university admission requirements and demonstrate academic excellence.
coverage: Full or partial tuition waiver, accommodation subsidy, monthly living allowance. Coverage varies by university and program.
required_documents: ["Passport copy","Academic certificates","Transcripts","Study plan","Recommendation letters","Language proficiency proof","Physical examination form"]
application_process:
1. Verify your country is part of Belt and Road Initiative
2. Select participating Chinese universities
3. Apply for university admission
4. Submit scholarship application to university
5. University reviews applications
6. Scholarship committee evaluates candidates
7. Results announced (usually 2-3 months after deadline)

name: University Scholarships
description: Individual Chinese universities offer their own scholarships to attract outstanding international students. These scholarships vary significantly by institution and are typically merit-based.
eligibility: Requirements vary by university. Generally based on academic merit, standardized test scores, and sometimes financial need. All degree levels eligible. Check individual university requirements.
coverage: Typically 30% to 100% tuition waiver. Some universities also provide accommodation subsidies or monthly stipends for exceptional students.
required_documents: ["Application form","Academic transcripts","Diplomas","Recommendation letters","Personal statement","Language test scores","Portfolio or achievements (if applicable)"]
application_process:
1. Research universities and their scholarship offerings
2. Check specific scholarship requirements on university website
3. Apply for admission to the university
4. Submit scholarship application (often included in admission application)
5. University admission office reviews applications
6. Scholarship decisions announced with admission results

name: MOFCOM Scholarship
description: The Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) Scholarship is designed for students from developing countries to pursue postgraduate studies in China, focusing on economic and commercial fields.
eligibility: Open to students from developing countries. Applicants must have bachelor degree for master programs or master degree for PhD programs. Age limit: under 45. Must have two years of work experience after graduation. Good health and valid passport required.
coverage: Full tuition waiver, free accommodation, comprehensive medical insurance, monthly stipend, one-time settling allowance
required_documents: ["Application form","Valid passport copy","Notarized diplomas","Academic transcripts","Work experience certificate","Two recommendation letters","Research proposal","Physical examination record","Language proficiency certificate"]
application_process:
1. Check if your country is eligible
2. Contact Chinese Embassy in your country
3. Obtain application materials from Embassy
4. Prepare all required documents
5. Submit application to Chinese Embassy
6. Embassy nominates candidates to MOFCOM
7. MOFCOM reviews and announces results
8. Receive admission notice from designated university

description:

Application: Directed to the local Chinese Embassy or Consulate.

Application Window: Usually November to April.

Requirement: Applicants should choose Type A and select Beijing Institute of Technology as the first priority institution.

(2) Beijing Government Scholarship (Only for Beijing campus)
Application deadline: May 1, 2026

Coverage Table:

Type A: Tuition (Full), University Accommodation, Stipend, Comprehensive Insurance.

Type B: Tuition (Full), University Accommodation, Comprehensive Insurance.

Type C: Tuition (Full), Comprehensive Insurance.

(3) Guangdong Government Outstanding International Student Scholarship (Only for Zhuhai campus)
Funding standard: CNY 10,000/year.

Application deadline: May 1, 2026.

(4) BIT International Student Scholarship
Application deadline: May 1, 2026.

Coverage Levels:

Four Year Duration:

First Prize: Full Tuition, Accommodation, Stipend, and Insurance.

Second Prize: Full Tuition and Accommodation.
